title: GrowGeneration Corp (GRWG) Q1 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
text: GrowGeneration reported challenging Q1 2022 results, with revenue declining 9% year-over-year to $81.8 million. Same-store sales plummeted 35%, while gross margins contracted to 27.1%, down 110 basis points from the prior year. The company posted a net loss of $5.2 million ($0.09 per share) compared to net income of $6.1 million in Q1 2021. Adjusted EBITDA swung to a loss of $700,000 from $11.1 million in the prior-year quarter. Management attributed weakness to significant cannabis oversupply in key markets. CEO Darren Lampert explained that demand for hydroponics remains soft, with a large oversupply of cannabis in the consumer marketplace. The issue particularly affects California, Oklahoma, and Michigan, representing over 55% of revenue. Capital expenditure projects have largely halted, with durable goods like lighting and HVAC equipment experiencing sharply reduced demand from large commercial accounts. GrowGeneration reduced new store opening targets from 15-20 to 10-15 locations, strategically focusing on emerging markets including Mississippi, Missouri, New Jersey, New York, and Virginia. The company maintains solid financial footing with $66 million in cash and zero debt. GrowGeneration revised 2022 guidance downward, projecting revenue between $340-$400 million and adjusted EBITDA above breakeven but below $10 million.
